spec.description: Example Lab CrowdSec Fleet Dashboard — alerts, decisions, scenarios, parsers, bouncers, per-machine breakdown. Tailored for gatekeeper LAPI at 192.168.70.84:6060.

spec.title: Alert POSTs per Agent (24h rate)
spec.description: Shows which host in your 11-node fleet is generating the most alerts. Machine IDs correspond to your agent hostnames.

spec.title: Total Active Alerts
spec.description: cs_alerts is a gauge of currently tracked alert reasons — not a counter. This shows total distinct active alert entries.

spec.title: Buckets Underflowed (Expired without Alert)
spec.description: High underflow relative to overflow means attacks that didn't quite reach threshold — normal for some scenarios.

spec.title: Parser Hits (OK vs KO)
spec.description: KO = lines that failed to parse. High KO rate means a log format mismatch or missing parser.

spec.title: Parse Success Rate
spec.description: Below 80% = investigate your parser/acquisition config. The red 648 you see on the existing dashboard is parsed lines count, not an error — check this gauge instead.

spec.title: Whitelisted Lines
spec.description: Lines matching your trusted-ips-whitelist and other whitelist parsers. High values here are expected and good — your fleet IPs being correctly whitelisted.

spec.title: Process Max FDs
spec.description: 524287 is the Linux kernel default ulimit (2^19 - 1). This is normal — not an error. Only worry if it hits the limit.

spec.title: Bouncer Decision Stream Pull Count
spec.description: Total /v1/decisions/stream GET calls per bouncer since last restart. All bouncers should be incrementing.

spec.title: Agent Alert Push Count
spec.description: Total POST /v1/alerts calls per machine agent. Agents that have detected nothing will show only heartbeat calls.

spec.title: Open FDs
spec.description: Actual open file descriptors vs max. Should be well below Max FDs.

options.content:
## About the values in your existing CrowdSec dashboards

**Process Max FDs = 524287 (red)** — This is **normal**. It is the Linux kernel default `ulimit -n` value (2^19 - 1 = 524287). It is the *maximum allowed* open file descriptors, not the current count. The threshold on your existing dashboard is incorrectly set too low. This panel above sets the threshold at 900,000 (near-limit) so it only alerts when you actually have a problem.

**Total Parsed Lines = 648 (red)** — The color is a dashboard threshold misconfiguration, not an error. 648 parsed lines in the last 15 minutes is healthy activity. Check the **Parse Success Rate** gauge above — if it's >95% you're fine. The red color means the existing dashboard has a threshold of 0 which marks anything >0 as red (inverted logic).

**Average CPU = No data** — The existing dashboard queries `process_cpu_seconds_total` which requires the `full` Prometheus level in CrowdSec config. Check `/etc/crowdsec/config.yaml` under `prometheus:` — if `level: aggregated` change to `level: full` and restart.

spec.title: Example Lab — CrowdSec Fleet
